pr.title: Split the remainder of .data and move declarations into C where possible
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Removed the monolithic asm/data.s .data blob and redistributed remaining data into module-owned assembly files under asm/dolphin/ax and asm/sysdolphin/baselib, while moving several now-understood declarations and strings into C. Build metadata was adjusted so data-bearing asm files are linked with the normal object list instead of a separate DATA_O_FILES bucket. The PR also replaced some extern lbl_* references with named C definitions such as hsdObj, hsdClass, and hsdDObj, and made HSD_ArchiveParse compile as C after resolving the prior string-section blocker.
what_changed: Deleted asm/data.s, which previously held a single 46661-line .data section from 0x803B9840 to 0x804316C0.

Added .data sections to many owning assembly files, with the largest blocks going into asm/sysdolphin/baselib/code_803A949C.s, asm/sysdolphin/baselib/code_80391580.s, asm/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib.s, and asm/dolphin/ax/AXAlloc.s.
Removed DATA_O_FILES from the Makefile O_FILES composition and deleted the DATA_O_FILES := $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/data.o definition from obj_files.mk.

Added module-specific asm objects such as asm/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.o and asm/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.o to TEXT_O_FILES where needed, and changed asm/sysdolphin/baselib/sobj.o to asm/sysdolphin/baselib/sobjlib.o.
Changed the epilogue build rules so %.s files in EPILOGUE_PROCESS are assembled directly with $(AS) and $(ASFLAGS), instead of treating all epilogue objects as C-family Frank post-processing inputs.
Moved known globals into C: HSD_ClassInfo hsdClass = { func_803822C0 }, HSD_ClassInfo hsdObj = { ObjInfoInit }, and HSD_DObjInfo hsdDObj = { DObjInfoInit }.
Renamed the commented variable entry in include/variables.h from lbl_804072A8 to hsdObj.
Replaced several extern string labels with direct C string literals or __FILE__ usage in class.c, gobjuserdata.c, memory.c, and object.c.
In aobj.c, replaced extern data labels lbl_80405FB8 and lbl_80405FC4 with explicit char arrays for "object.h" and "HSD_OBJ(o)->ref_count != HSD_OBJ_NOREF".
Removed the inline asm fallback for HSD_ArchiveParse; the C implementation became the active implementation after the string extern/section problem was eliminated.
Removed the update function pointer from HSD_DObjInfo in dobj.h, indicating the struct layout was corrected or narrowed by evidence in this slice.
smart_moves: Splitting .data by owner makes future decompilation more searchable and keeps data near the code that references it, while preserving unknown labels where names were not yet justified.

Moving understood globals and assertion strings into C reduced dependence on anonymous lbl_* externs and enabled at least one previously non-matching function path, HSD_ArchiveParse, to be expressed in C.
The build-system change removed the special asm/data.o link bucket instead of leaving dead infrastructure behind, making link order reflect per-module ownership.

The PR only named symbols when there was direct evidence from surrounding C usage, for example hsdObj, hsdClass, hsdDObj, and known assertion/file strings; unknown jump tables and data stayed as jtbl_* or lbl_*.
Adding a direct assembly rule for epilogue %.s outputs avoided running Frank/C compiler post-processing on pure assembly data splits.
decomp_lessons: When a giant .data file is split, update both object lists and build recipes; otherwise link order and epilogue processing can silently diverge from the original binary.
Known assertion strings can often be moved from extern lbl_* declarations into literal C arguments, which may unblock matching by avoiding section-placement constraints.

Class/object singleton data such as hsdClass, hsdObj, and hsdDObj should be defined in the C file that owns initialization logic when evidence is clear, instead of being treated as anonymous data labels.

Deleting inline asm fallbacks is safest when the root mismatch cause is removed; here the slice explicitly notes the old archive.c non-match was because the string could not be externed due to section placement.
Data ownership can be incremental: keep unresolved arrays, jump tables, and opaque data in per-module asm while migrating high-confidence declarations to C.
Header layout changes should be small and evidence-driven; dobj.h removed a trailing function pointer rather than broadly rewriting the type.
naming_conventions: Use real baselib singleton names where known: hsdClass, hsdObj, hsdDObj.
Retain lbl_80xxxxxx names for unresolved data labels and jtbl_80xxxxxx for jump tables.
Prefer direct C string literals or __FILE__ for assert file/condition strings once their contents are known.
Reflect newly named data in include/variables.h comments, as shown by lbl_804072A8 becoming hsdObj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Move .data from asm/data.s into the assembly file for the subsystem or source module that owns the references.
Append module-local .section .data blocks after existing text assembly while preserving labels, alignment, .global declarations, and raw .4byte/.asciz contents.
Keep TEXT_O_FILES ordered to preserve binary layout after adding per-module asm data objects.
Remove DATA_O_FILES once the monolithic data object is gone, so there is no separate data-only link phase.
For epilogue builds, assemble %.s files directly with AS/ASFLAGS and reserve Frank processing for C inputs with a corresponding build object.
Use C definitions for known globals and strings to solve section-related matching blockers before deleting inline asm fallbacks.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review body were present in the provided slice; no explicit reviewer feedback is captured.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue replacing lbl_* data symbols with hsd* class/object singleton names.
Search for remaining DATA_O_FILES or asm/data.s references after PR 218.
Audit baselib .s files added in this PR for data blocks that can now be moved into C.
Check later changes to HSD_DObjInfo to confirm the removed update field and final struct layout.
Search for inline asm fallbacks guarded by NON_MATCHING that only exist because of extern string or data-section placement issues.
